svg.text: NIST SP 800-61 · SANS PICERL · ISO 27035 · GDPR/NIS2/DORA · Digital Forensics · Chain of Custody
Network block at perimeter + DNS sinkhole
• Hash every artifact at collection: SHA-256
• Verify hash on every transfer / access
• Never work on originals — forensic copies only
Update freq: every 30 min on P1 · 2h on P2
• Media: PR only — no analyst statements
• GDPR clock starts at time of awareness, not time of attack
• Report even when uncertain whether personal data was accessed
• NIS2 requires early warning in 24h, full notification in 72h (two separate reports)
• GDPR Art. 34: notify individuals too when there is high risk to their rights/freedoms
• Maintain breach register (GDPR Art. 33.5) — even for non-notifiable incidents
⚠ Consult DPO and legal counsel before filing regulatory notifications
Key Tools & NIST vs SANS Comparison
Disk: FTK Imager, Autopsy, dd, dcfldd, KAPE
Security Operations Master Class · Session 10 · Incident Response and Management
